candidate_model_parameters.Calibration object: Historical variance state plus option variance multiplier
candidate_model_parameters.Pricing method: Moment-matched stochastic variance with Merton jumps
Black-Scholes with active-regime fitted IV surface

candidate_model_parameters.Calibration object: Weighted option-price error with mean-reverting lambda
candidate_model_parameters.Pricing method: Merton mixture with horizon-dependent effective intensity
Black-Scholes with GARCH forecast average variance

candidate_model_parameters.Calibration object: Historical variance state plus option variance multiplier
candidate_model_parameters.Pricing method: Moment-matched stochastic variance with Merton jumps
Black-Scholes with active-regime fitted IV surface

candidate_model_parameter_details.description: ATM IV level (baseline implied volatility)
linear skew slope (tilts smile left/right)
symmetric smile width (V-shape lift)
quadratic smile curvature (bowl depth)
term-structure level (IV change with sqrtT)
term-structure curvature (IV change with T)
put vs call IV offset at ATM
put-side skew slope (asymmetric tilt)
short-dated IV premium (<=30 day options)
SVI cross-term: skew steepness vs maturity
put-side term slope (separate from calls)
cubic skew asymmetry (OTM put wing steepness)
minimum predicted IV (crypto lower bound)
maximum predicted IV (outlier cap)
in-sample relative price RMSE from surface calibration
initial variance v0 (spot variance at calibration date)
long-run variance theta (variance mean-reversion target)
variance mean-reversion speed kappa (per year; higher = faster pull to theta)
mean variance jump size mu_v (Exp scale; adds to v on each jump)
spot-vol correlation rho (negative = leverage effect / skew)
overall variance scale factor (calibrated to option prices)
jump arrival rate lambda (jumps per year)
mean log spot-price jump mu_S (negative = crash bias)
std of log spot-price jump sigma_S (uncertainty in jump size)
vol-of-vol xi (controls variance-of-variance / smile width)
diffusive volatility sigma (non-jump component)
long-run jump intensity lambda (unconditional arrival rate per year)
current jump intensity lambda_t (elevated after recent jumps)
intensity mean-reversion speed beta (per year; how fast lambda_t -> lambda)
mean log spot-price jump mu_S
std of log spot-price jump sigma_S
GARCH constant omega (unconditional variance floor)
ARCH coefficient alpha (weight on last squared return)
GARCH coefficient beta (weight on last variance)
conditional variance at last observation h_T
estimated mean log-return (subtracted before fitting)
trading periods per year used for annualisation
GARCH persistence (< 1 required for stationarity; crypto approx 0.95-0.99)
unconditional variance implied by GARCH parameters
